name: Spaghetti Sauce with Meat
recipeInstructions:
Yield: Approximately 9 pints
Procedure:
1. Prepare tomatoes according to the directions for Spaghetti Sauce Without Meat.
2. Cook ground beef or sausage until browned.
3. Add garlic, onions, celery (or green peppers), and mushrooms (if desired).
4. Cook vegetables until they are tender.
5. Combine with tomato pulp in a large saucepan.
6. Add spices, salt, and sugar; stir well.
7. Bring to a boil and simmer, uncovered, until thick enough for serving. The initial volume will reduce by nearly half during cooking.
8. Stir frequently to prevent burning.
9. Fill jars, leaving 1-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and process according to recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2 based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for Spaghetti Sauce With Meat in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
Pack Style: Hot
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Processing Time: 60 minutes for Pints; 70 minutes for Quarts
Canner Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb
4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb
6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb
Table 2: Recommended processing time for Spaghetti Sauce With Meat in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
Pack Style: Hot
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Processing Time: 60 minutes for Pints; 70 minutes for Quarts
Canner Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
